title: Bankruptcy proceedings commenced
description: Tokyo District Court commenced bankruptcy proceedings for MtGox Co., Ltd. on April 24, 2014.

title: Chapter 11 filing and collapse
description: FTX and affiliated debtors filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on November 11, 2022.

title: Binance launched
description: Binance began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: CCAA protection and insolvency crisis
description: Quadriga sought and received CCAA protection on February 5, 2019 after the platform had shut down on January 28, 2019.

title: FinCEN penalties and U.S. enforcement action
description: FinCEN assessed penalties against BTC-e on July 26, 2017 as U.S. enforcement action escalated.

title: January 2019 exchange hack
description: Official liquidation materials describe a serious January 2019 hack that caused severe operational and reputational damage.
notes: The official source confirms the hack occurred in January 2019, but not the precise day.

title: Liquidators appointed and trading suspended
description: Cryptopia reached an operational end state associated with hack.

title: Operations wound down
description: Global exchange brand from the broader Bittrex lineage, later wound down after regulatory pressure and liquidation proceedings.

title: Operations ceased
description: Centralized exchange founded in 2018 that ceased CEX operations in May 2023 after deteriorating operating conditions and earlier disruption tied to an investigation.

title: Ownership changed through acquisition
description: Poloniex underwent an acquisition-related ownership change.
notes: Ownership transition only; exchange continued operating.

title: Vaultoro launched
description: Vaultoro began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Platform migration underway
description: Official messaging described a temporary migration with customer assets stated to remain safe.
notes: Official site showed a migration/update notice in April 2026 with customer assets stated to remain safe. Launch date uses the May 2015 public launch reference for the trading service; company/about material also claims operations since 2014.

title: Cryptsy launched
description: Cryptsy began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Insolvency crisis became definitive
description: Cryptsy reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Later reporting places the exchange launch in May 2013. The operational end marker uses the January 2016 disclosure of hidden losses and withdrawal failure.

title: BitGrail launched
description: BitGrail began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to year level.

title: Operations ceased
description: BitGrail ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Founding year is treated as 2017 from retrospective reporting. Death marker uses the Florence court order to cease operations on 2018-05-03; the primary collapse trigger remained the February 2018 Nano loss.

title: Operations ceased
description: Livecoin ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch uses the specific April 2015 operating-since statement from the shutdown coverage. Some secondary profiles cite earlier founding dates; these are left in notes rather than used as the operational launch marker.

title: Operations ceased
description: MapleChange ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Public reporting conflicts on whether the terminal crisis is better characterized as a hack or an exit scam.

title: Cobinhood platform went live
description: Cobinhood began operating as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange platform.
notes: HEI uses the 2017-12-18 live-platform report as the launch marker.

title: Cobinhood was moved to dead-side handling after shutdown notice
description: Cobinhood reached its terminal HEI operating state after the 2020-01-10 shutdown and account-balance audit notice.
notes: The notice described an audit period and later reopening for users to retrieve funds; HEI uses the first shutdown notice as the terminal marker while keeping the cause unknown.

title: Altsbit launched
description: Altsbit began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Operations ceased
description: Altsbit ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Public coverage indicates the venue was only a few months old by February 2020. The operational end marker uses the published refund deadline in May 2020.

title: Operations wound down
description: WEX reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses the September 2017 rebrand/launch announcement. Death marker uses the late-November 2018 domain-loss point as an operational end marker; exact final shutdown timing remains contested.

title: Operations wound down
description: BX Thailand reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.

title: Operations interrupted
description: COSS went offline and then entered an acquisition/relaunch process, leaving long-term status ambiguous.
notes: COSS was taken offline on 2020-01-07 and later described acquisition negotiations and a relaunch plan. Because the record is too ambiguous for a clean dead-side classification, it is moved to inactive pending deeper verification.

title: Vircurex launched
description: Vircurex began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Insolvency crisis became definitive
description: Vircurex reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses repeated October 2011 references. Operational end marker uses the March 2014 withdrawal freeze and account-freeze period, which followed earlier hacks and a reserve crisis.

title: Operations ceased
description: CoinsMarkets ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch remains approximate because strong primary founding records are still missing. Operational end marker uses the point when users widely reported trading and withdrawals disabled on 2018-01-05.

title: MintPal launched
description: MintPal began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Operations ceased
description: MintPal ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch uses a specific February 2014 launch reference. Death marker uses 2014-10-14 when Moolah announced MintPal had been shut down while preparing bankruptcy; later fallout continued after that date.

title: Vault of Satoshi launched
description: Vault of Satoshi began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Operations wound down
description: Vault of Satoshi reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses October 2013 reporting on the exchange going live. Closure date and reason come from the January 2015 shutdown announcement specifying a voluntary end rather than insolvency or theft.

title: Poloniex spins out from Circle
description: Poloniex leadership spun the exchange out of Circle into Polo Digital Assets, Ltd., continuing operations outside Circle.
notes: Ownership / corporate structure change; not a death-side terminal event.

title: Trading and wallet use suspended
description: LocalBitcoins suspended trading and normal wallet use on 2023-02-16, leaving withdrawals only afterward.
notes: Closure was announced on 2023-02-09; this event uses the effective service-stop date.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the Coinsetter acquisition on 2016-01-19 and stated that client accounts would be transferred on 2016-01-26.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-01-26 transfer date.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the Cavirtex acquisition on 2016-01-19 and stated that client accounts would be transferred on 2016-01-26.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-01-26 transfer date.

title: BitBay rebranded to Zonda
description: BitBay announced on 2021-11-08 that the exchange brand had changed to Zonda, with legacy BitBay platform URLs later redirecting to Zonda domains.
notes: Further downstream rebranding to zondacrypto should be handled as successor lineage, not silent overwrite.

title: BITFRONT service terminated
description: BITFRONT's closure notice wound down service in stages and ended withdrawals and remaining customer access on 2023-03-31.
notes: The initial shutdown announcement was published on 2022-11-28.

title: OPNX ceased operations
description: OPNX told users to withdraw funds and export historical data by 2024-02-14 as part of its February 2024 shutdown.
notes: Shutdown date is supported by strong reporting; launch lineage traces to the March 2023 CoinFLEX-to-OPNX rebrand.

title: Youbit halted trading and filed for bankruptcy
description: Youbit stopped trading and entered bankruptcy proceedings after disclosing a second 2017 hack that reduced customer balances.
notes: The retained marker combines the service halt and bankruptcy filing date used in same-day reporting.

title: Gatecoin ordered to wind up and cease operations
description: Gatecoin ceased operations after a Hong Kong court granted a winding-up order against the company, leading to compulsory liquidation.
notes: Liquidators were formally appointed on 2019-03-20; HEI keeps 2019-03-13 as the operational stop and terminal marker.

title: Koinex permanently disabled trading
description: Koinex permanently disabled digital-asset trading on all platforms on 2019-06-27 and moved to withdrawals-only wind-down.
notes: The later withdrawal deadline of 2019-07-15 is kept as supporting context, not the entity death marker.

title: CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor customer base and domains
description: CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor's customer base and domains in October 2021 and began migrating or closing legacy Coinfloor accounts.
notes: Further lineage work should attach the successor relationship once CoinCorner is modeled in canonical data.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the CleverCoin acquisition on 2016-06-28 and said customer accounts would be automatically transferred the next day.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-06-29 transfer completion date.

title: Coinnest shutdown process began
description: Coinnest posted a closure notice saying it was no longer operating as of Tuesday and set an April 30 deadline for withdrawals.
notes: The retained event date follows the effective service-stop marker referenced in reporting on April 18–19, 2019.

title: Liqui announced shutdown and 30 day withdrawal window
description: Liqui announced it was shutting down on 2019-01-28 and told users they had 30 days to withdraw funds.
notes: Shutdown rationale quoted in reporting focused on insufficient liquidity and lack of economic justification for continued service.

title: Nova ceased trading and API access
description: Nova said trading would cease and the API would be deactivated on 2019-10-07 as part of its shutdown schedule.
notes: Withdrawals were left open until 2019-10-25, but the operational end marker used here is the trading/API stop date.

title: Interim receiver secured Einstein Exchange
description: The Supreme Court of British Columbia appointed an interim receiver on 2019-11-01, which then secured the premises and shut down platform access.
notes: BCSC said platform counsel had told regulators on 2019-10-31 that the business planned to shut down within 30 to 60 days due to a lack of profit.

title: The Rock Trading interrupted platform operations
description: The Rock Trading said it had to interrupt operation of its platform because of difficulties encountered in liquidity management.
notes: The operational stop preceded judicial liquidation and insolvency proceedings in Milan.

title: VirWoX permanently closed its service
description: VirWoX notified users in late 2019 that the service would permanently close on 2020-01-06 and account access would end that day.
notes: The retained date follows the stated end-of-service date rather than the earlier announcement date.

title: Bitcurex went offline after reported external interference
description: Bitcurex reported asset losses and went offline in October 2016.
notes: Followed by a prosecutor investigation in 2017.

title: Tradehill halted trading again
description: Tradehill halted trading in August 2013 because of banking and regulatory issues.
notes: Earlier 2012 shutdown remains contextual evidence.

title: Coin.mx operators were arrested and charged
description: U.S. authorities charged Coin.mx operators with running an unlawful bitcoin exchange and money-laundering scheme.
notes: Enforcement-side operational end marker.

title: Intersango closed its remaining GBP market
description: Intersango progressively shut markets during 2012 and closed its remaining GBP market in December.
notes: Uses the final GBP closure date.

title: Binance Uganda fully shut down
description: Binance Uganda announced a phased wind-down and fully shut down on 2020-11-28.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: Mt. Gox acquired Bitomat
description: After Bitomat lost wallet data and customer balances, Mt. Gox acquired the service and migrated users in August 2011.
notes: Successor is Mt. Gox.

title: Cointrader closed after alleged hack
description: Cointrader said it had closed its doors effective immediately after a debilitating hack.
notes: Immediate shutdown notice.

title: Yunbi announced closure of trading operations
description: Yunbi said it would close trading during the 2017 China crackdown on exchanges.
notes: China crackdown closure.

title: Binance Jersey fully shut down
description: Binance Jersey wound down through November 2020 and fully shut down on 2020-11-30.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: Binance Singapore ended DPT services
description: Binance.sg shut down by 2022-02-13 after the operator withdrew its licensing effort.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: ACH and wire transfers halted
description: CampBX halted ACH and wire transfers after its bank decided not to work with bitcoin companies because of regulatory uncertainty.
notes: Earlier operational stress marker, not the final terminal event.

title: Georgia cease and desist became final
description: The Georgia Department of Banking and Finance said an order to cease and desist against Bulbul Investments LLC doing business as CampBX became final on 2018-07-25.
notes: Used as the terminal marker for public-ready handling.

title: CryptoBridge shutdown announced
description: CryptoBridge announced it would close because of market conditions and increasing regulation.
notes: Deposits were to close on 2019-12-03.

title: CryptoBridge services terminated
description: CryptoBridge said all services and servers would terminate after 2019-12-15.
notes: HEI uses 2019-12-15 as the terminal marker.

title: Coinroom entered the KNF warning list
description: Coinroom was added to the Polish KNF public warning list over suspected unlicensed payment-services activity.
notes: Important regulatory precursor, but not the final terminal event.

title: Coinroom shut down
description: Users reported receiving account-termination notices and Coinroom ceased operating in early April 2019.
notes: Cause remains conservative because later reporting mixes regulatory and fund-loss frames.

title: Bitsane withdrawals began failing
description: Users reported that Bitsane withdrawals began failing in May 2019, with support citing temporary technical reasons before the exchange later disappeared.
notes: Start-of-failure marker uses month-level dating from aligned reporting.

title: Bitsane website and social accounts disappeared
description: By 2019-06-17, the Bitsane website and social media accounts had disappeared after weeks of withdrawal failures, and public reporting later treated the case as an apparent exit scam.
notes: HEI uses the 2019-06-17 disappearance date as the terminal marker while preserving the later 2019-06-27 public inactive / scam-style reporting in evidence.

title: Cold wallet hack
description: Bter said 7,170 BTC were stolen from its cold wallet and the platform was temporarily shut down.
notes: Major historical incident, but not the final terminal event.

title: Bter users migrated to Gate.io
description: Gate.io announced support for BTER users to move funds to gate.io, reflecting the completed brand transition.
notes: HEI uses 2017-12-31 as the conservative rebrand marker.

title: Abucoins entered the KNF warning list
description: The Polish KNF added Abucoins sp. z o.o. to its public warnings list over suspected unauthorized payment-services activity.
notes: Important regulatory precursor, not the final terminal event.

title: Abucoins closed down
description: By 2019-02-26, Abucoins was publicly marked closed and inactive.
notes: Uses the 2019-02-26 closure marker from public exchange-status sources.

title: Cryptonit marked closed
description: By 2019-04-01, Cryptonit was publicly described as having closed down operations.
notes: Conservative terminal marker from public exchange-status sources.

title: Operations closed
description: A public update stated that Cryptonit had closed down operations, referencing an announcement email to customers.
notes: HEI uses 2019-04-01 as the terminal date.

title: C-CEX became inaccessible
description: By 2019-06-27, public exchange-status sources reported that the website was not accessible.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: C-CEX marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked C-CEX as dead or closed by 2019-06-27.
notes: HEI uses 2019-06-27 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Coinbase announced the transition away from Coinbase Pro

notes: Announcement month used conservatively from official help text.

title: Coinbase Pro discontinued
description: Coinbase documentation states Coinbase Pro was discontinued in December 2023 and replaced by Coinbase Advanced.
notes: HEI uses 2023-12-31 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: BTC38 site failed to load

notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: BTC38 marked dead

notes: HEI uses 2019-06-12 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: LakeBTC site became inaccessible

title: LakeBTC marked dead

notes: HEI uses 2021-07-15 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: ACX site became inaccessible

notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: NIX-E marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked NIX-E as dead by 2019-07-18.
notes: HEI uses 2019-07-18 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: ZT.com site became inaccessible

title: ZT.com Exchange marked dead

title: SushiSwap launched in August 2020

title: TradeOgre was dismantled and effectively shut down

description: The Frankfurt public prosecutor's cybercrime unit and the BKA seized the Germany-based server infrastructure of eXch and confiscated cryptocurrency assets, with support from the Dutch FIOD.

title: AllCrypt was moved to dead-side handling after the hack
description: After the March 2015 compromise, AllCrypt went offline and is now listed as inactive by exchange data references.
notes: HEI uses the public hack/site-offline marker as the terminal date rather than a later unstated final domain-offline date.

title: BitBay rebranded to Zonda
description: The exchange previously known as BitBay moved into the Zonda brand in November 2021, with user login data and platform continuity described as unchanged.
notes: Month/day marker follows public reporting of the 2021 BitBay-to-Zonda rebrand.

title: Zonda brand moved to zondacrypto
description: The Zonda exchange brand later moved to the zondacrypto name; HEI treats BitBay / Zonda / zondacrypto as the same lineage unless later evidence requires a split.
notes: The exact source should be upgraded to an accessible official archived page if available.

title: Polish prosecutors opened a Zondacrypto investigation
description: The Regional Prosecutor's Office in Katowice opened an investigation concerning Zondacrypto, including suspected misleading of users about the purchase and storage of fiat currencies and cryptocurrencies, possible money-laundering-related conduct, reported withdrawal problems, and lack of access to an approximately 4,500 BTC cold wallet.
notes: Official prosecutor material reports damage of no less than 350 million PLN and states that the investigation concerns conduct from 2022 onward.

title: Victim instructions referenced failed or partial withdrawal requests
description: The prosecutor's office published instructions for potential victims, asking them to document when and in what amount they ordered withdrawals from Zondacrypto, including withdrawals that were not fully or partially executed.
notes: This is modeled as withdrawal_suspended because official victim instructions and investigation materials document withdrawal failures, but HEI should avoid claiming a final global service shutdown until stronger official confirmation appears.

title: CEO reportedly located in Israel during crisis
description: Onet reported that zondacrypto CEO Przemyslaw Kral was in Israel while the exchange was described as on the edge of bankruptcy and while the 4,500 BTC reserve-key dispute remained unresolved.

title: CoinMKT public beta launched

title: CoinPulse suspended trading and deposits

title: Bit.com spot trading scheduled to end

title: Drift suspended deposits and withdrawals

title: Gemini closed customer accounts in selected regions

title: ProBit Global announced full service termination
description: ProBit Global announced a complete service wind-down, citing a rapidly evolving regulatory environment and strategic business restructuring.

title: ProBit Global spot trading ended
description: ProBit Global's shutdown roadmap ended spot trading and delisted all spot pairs on January 28, 2026.

title: Grinex reported a major cyberattack

notes: Use hack rather than exploit because reporting describes a cyberattack on an exchange wallet / operations environment rather than a smart-contract exploit.

title: Grinex withdrawals and operations were suspended
description: Reporting described Grinex as suspending operations after the attack, with follow-on coverage describing halted trading and blocked withdrawals during the investigation.

title: Grinex was sanctioned by multiple jurisdictions
description: Reuters reported that Grinex had been sanctioned by the United States, United Kingdom, and European Union in 2025, with U.S. authorities linking it to sanctions-evasion infrastructure involving the A7A5 ruble-backed stablecoin.

title: Rhea Finance suffered a major exploit
description: Rhea Finance suffered an exploit involving fake tokens, liquidity-pool manipulation, and oracle / validation or slippage-protection weakness; early reports estimated USD 7.6 million stolen, while later analysis described USD 18.4 million before recovery or freezes.

title: Rhea Finance withdrawals were halted
description: Reporting stated that withdrawals were halted while the Rhea team worked to contain further damage after the exploit.
notes: Keep limited until recovery or permanent shutdown evidence appears.

title: Bunni paused smart contracts after exploit
description: Bunni paused all smart-contract functions after an exploit drained an estimated USD 8.4 million across supported networks, including Ethereum and Unichain exposure.

title: Bunni announced shutdown after the exploit

title: Bunni treated as permanently closed

title: eXch announced it would cease operations
description: eXch announced that it would cease operations on May 1, 2025, describing a management decision to cease and retreat in the hostile legal and surveillance environment.

title: eXch shutdown date reached

title: BigONE suffered a USD 27 million breach

title: BigONE deposits were temporarily suspended
description: BigONE temporarily suspended deposits while reinforcing system security after the breach, with later updates indicating deposit and trading services were restored.

description: Reporting described BigONE as temporarily freezing or suspending trading functions after the USD 27 million security incident while working with SlowMist to trace stolen funds.
notes: Temporary halt; keep entity active unless later evidence shows unresolved service failure.

title: Balancer v2 pools suffered a major exploit
description: Balancer v2 pools were exploited across multiple chains in November 2025, with secondary reports describing approximately USD 128 million drained and the flaw later cited as a source of legal exposure for Balancer Labs.
notes: Use exploit rather than hack because reporting attributes the loss to a protocol-level flaw in Balancer v2 logic.

title: Balancer Labs announced it would wind down

notes: Corporate entity wind-down only. Do not treat as protocol death.

title: Polynomial Protocol announced final wind-down
description: Polynomial announced the closure of Polynomial Chain and Polynomial Trade, cancellation of its planned Q1 2026 TGE, and a phased wind-down including forced liquidation, liquidity-layer closure, and chain shutdown.
notes: Announcement date follows syndicated PANews / exchange-news reporting.

title: Polynomial forced-liquidation phase began

title: Polynomial Chain shutdown date reached
description: The final timeline for Polynomial's wind-down scheduled chain operations to halt on March 3, 2026, after liquidity-layer closure on February 24.

title: Step Finance suffered a major treasury breach

notes: Loss amounts vary; avoid a single definitive figure in entity-level fields.

title: Step Finance announced group wind-down
description: Step Finance announced that Step Finance, SolanaFloor, and Remora Markets would wind down after failing to secure financing, acquisition, or another viable recovery path following the breach.
notes: Announcement date follows the project statement as reported by The Block, Cointelegraph, and other sources.

title: Step Finance treated as closed

title: Cetus Protocol suffered a major exploit

notes: Loss amount varies across sources; use approximate language.

title: Cetus halted trading and paused contracts
description: Cetus halted trading / paused smart-contract activity while investigating the exploit and coordinating recovery through Sui validators, the Sui Foundation, and security partners.
notes: Temporary platform halt; not a terminal shutdown.

title: Cetus relaunched after recovery plan
description: Cetus relaunched on June 8, 2025 after recovery work, external audits, frozen-fund transfer approval, a Sui Foundation loan, and liquidity restoration for affected pools.
notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no dedicated restart/relaunch enum.

title: KiloEx suffered a price-oracle exploit

notes: Use exploit because the incident exploited oracle pricing logic rather than a general exchange-wallet breach.

title: KiloEx operations were paused after the exploit
description: KiloEx paused operations / trading for roughly ten days while preparing recovery and compensation measures after the oracle-manipulation attack.
notes: Temporary service disruption; not a shutdown.

title: KiloEx announced compensation and resolution plan
description: KiloEx published a resolution plan to compensate users with open positions and address trading-position imbalances before service resumption.
notes: Compensation/resolution context supporting active rather than dead classification.

title: Bybit announced Japan resident service restrictions
description: Bybit announced that it would discontinue services for Japanese residents and gradually apply account restrictions from 2026 in response to Japanese regulatory-compliance requirements, after earlier suspension of new Japan registrations.
notes: Regional exit only; Bybit global entity remains active.

title: Bybit Japan user verification deadline reached
description: Affected users were instructed to complete Identity Verification Level 2 / proof-of-address checks by January 22, 2026 or be classified as Japanese residents and face service limitations.
notes: Modeled as other because this is a regional KYC / residency-classification milestone rather than a global exchange status event.

title: OKX paused its DEX aggregator after Lazarus misuse concerns
description: OKX temporarily suspended its decentralized exchange aggregator after consulting regulators, citing efforts to prevent further misuse by Lazarus-linked activity and to apply security upgrades during heightened EU/MiCA scrutiny of its Web3 service.
notes: Product/service-level event only. OKX global exchange remains active.

title: OKX wallet service remained available while new wallet creation was limited in some markets
description: Reporting stated that OKX Wallet remained available to customers while new wallet creation was paused in selected markets as part of the service-review and upgrade response.
notes: Supporting service-scope event; not a full wallet or exchange shutdown.

title: Paradex pricing glitch triggered mass liquidations
description: A database migration / pricing-data error on Paradex briefly priced BTC at zero during maintenance, triggering mass unintended liquidations across perpetual markets.
notes: Use trading_halted because the incident affected trading, liquidation, and platform state.

title: Paradex performed a chain rollback
description: Paradex rolled back chain state to a prior block after the pricing-data incident, a rare and controversial response to a technical failure in a decentralized perpetuals platform.
notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no rollback-specific event enum.

title: Paradex refunded affected users after the rollback incident
description: Paradex said it refunded about USD 650,000 to around 200 users after the maintenance-related bug and unintended liquidations.
notes: Refund context supports active classification despite the critical incident.

title: SEC settled charges against Mango Markets entities
description: The SEC filed settled charges against Mango DAO, Blockworks Foundation, and Mango Labs over unregistered offers/sales of MNGO tokens and unregistered broker activity connected to the Mango Markets platform.
notes: Primary regulatory precursor to the 2025 wind-down.

title: Mango Markets announced it would shut down
description: Mango Markets announced that users should close positions and that Mango v4 / Boost were winding down, with governance proposals set to make most borrowing economically unviable.
notes: Announcement date follows reporting of Mango's public January 11 statement.

title: Mango Markets wind-down proposals became executable
description: Governance proposals tied to the wind-down became executable on January 13, 2025, effectively ending core borrowing/lending/trading viability on Mango v4 / Boost.
notes: HEI uses the proposal execution date as the terminal operational marker.

title: Nobitex suffered a politically motivated hack
description: Nobitex was attacked by hackers identifying as Predatory Sparrow / Gonjeshke Darande, with roughly USD 90 million in crypto assets transferred to inaccessible or burned addresses and source-code exposure threatened.
notes: The event is politically motivated and destructive rather than normal theft for profit, according to multiple sources.

title: Nobitex website and app were taken offline
description: Nobitex said it pulled its website and app offline while reviewing unauthorized access to its systems and hot wallet infrastructure after the attack.
notes: Modeled as withdrawal_suspended because site/app offline and system review imply service interruption, but not enough for dead classification.

title: WazirX suffered a USD 230-235 million hack
description: WazirX suffered a large hack in July 2024 that drained roughly USD 230-235 million from the platform and led to a long suspension and restructuring process.
notes: Loss amount varies slightly by source; keep approximate language.

title: WazirX halted withdrawals and trading after the breach
description: Following the July 2024 breach, WazirX suspended platform operations including withdrawals and trading, producing a roughly 15-16 month interruption before phased restart.
notes: This is the main service-interruption marker.

title: Singapore High Court sanctioned WazirX restructuring scheme
description: Zettai announced that Singapore's High Court sanctioned a creditor-approved restructuring scheme, paving the way for WazirX to restart operations within ten business days of effectiveness.
notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no restructuring-approved enum.

title: WazirX restarted trading after long suspension
description: WazirX restarted trading on October 24, 2025, roughly 15-16 months after the July 2024 hack, with phased market activation and zero-fee restart offers.
notes: Restart marker supports active classification; some reports noted withdrawals remained constrained during early restart.

title: Phemex detected a hot-wallet security incident
description: Phemex detected unusual activity in its hot wallet and described the incident as a hacker attack, isolating affected devices and contacting security firms and law enforcement.
notes: Official source is primary; external estimates of loss are not required for entity classification.

title: Phemex temporarily suspended deposits and withdrawals
description: Phemex suspended deposits and withdrawals across chains as an emergency response to the hot-wallet incident, then resumed chains and assets in staged updates.
notes: Official timeline gives asset-by-asset restoration dates.

title: Phemex withdrawal services were restored
description: Phemex's final update stated that all withdrawal services were restored in February 2025 after the hot-wallet incident response and system security upgrades.
notes: Month-level restoration marker based on official final update.

title: Upbit suffered a Solana hot-wallet incident
description: Upbit detected abnormal withdrawals of Solana-network assets, with reporting estimating roughly USD 30-40 million or 54 billion KRW in assets transferred to an unauthorized external wallet.
notes: Loss figures vary by source and exchange update; keep approximate range.

title: Upbit suspended deposits and withdrawals after abnormal activity
description: Upbit urgently suspended digital-asset deposits and withdrawals after detecting abnormal activity involving Solana-network tokens and moved remaining assets to cold wallets.
notes: Temporary suspension; not a death event.

title: Upbit scheduled Solana-related deposit and withdrawal resumption
description: Upbit announced that deposits and withdrawals for Solana network-related digital assets and SOL staking services would resume sequentially from December 4, 2025.
notes: Restoration marker supports active classification.

title: CoinDCX confirmed an internal operational-account hack
description: CoinDCX confirmed a security breach involving an internal operational account, with reports estimating about USD 44 million stolen; the company said customer funds were not affected.
notes: Use careful wording: sources report that an internal operational account was affected, while customer funds were said to be safe.

title: CoinDCX paused or limited some services after the breach
description: Reports described temporary product/service restrictions after the breach, including Web3 / INR and crypto-withdrawal handling, while CoinDCX worked to contain the incident and reassure users about fund safety.
notes: Modeled as withdrawal_suspended because the service impact centered on transfer/withdrawal handling rather than a permanent exchange halt.

title: CoinDCX resumed withdrawals after the breach
description: Indian business press reported that CoinDCX resumed withdrawals after the USD 44 million breach and reiterated that user funds were safe.
notes: Restoration marker supports active classification.

title: Bybit suffered a record-scale Ethereum wallet hack
description: Bybit suffered a record-scale Ethereum wallet hack in February 2025, with reports estimating about USD 1.4-1.5 billion stolen from a cold-wallet transfer/signing process while the exchange continued operating globally.
notes: Existing Bybit entity remains active. This event should not be interpreted as a global shutdown.

title: Bybit closed its ETH reserve gap after the hack
description: Bybit said or was reported to have closed the ETH gap after the hack by acquiring or securing large amounts of ETH, restoring 1:1 backing and continuing operations.
notes: Recovery/reserve-gap event supports active classification despite the critical hack.

title: DMM Bitcoin suffered an unauthorized BTC outflow
description: DMM Bitcoin suffered an unauthorized outflow of bitcoin in 2024, widely reported as approximately 4,502.9 BTC and roughly JPY 48 billion at the time.
notes: Use hack because the outflow is the causative security incident behind the later transfer and shutdown.

title: DMM Bitcoin announced customer transfer to SBI VC Trade
description: DMM Bitcoin announced an agreement to transfer customer accounts and assets to SBI VC Trade, with the transfer path replacing a restart of DMM Bitcoin's own exchange service.
notes: This is the practical shutdown-announcement marker; use official DMM/SBI sources for final staging validation.

title: DMM Bitcoin service ended after SBI VC Trade transfer
description: DMM Bitcoin service ended on March 8, 2025 after customer assets and accounts were transferred to SBI VC Trade.
notes: HEI uses the official service-end / transfer completion date as the terminal marker.

title: BtcTurk saw suspicious hot-wallet outflows
description: BtcTurk experienced suspicious outflows from hot wallets in August 2025, with blockchain/security reporting and news sources estimating the affected value around USD 48-49 million.
notes: Use careful language because some sources frame it as suspicious outflows / hack fears, while the exchange described security measures and user-asset safety.

title: BtcTurk suspended crypto deposits and withdrawals
description: BtcTurk suspended cryptocurrency deposits and withdrawals after the suspicious hot-wallet activity, while reports indicated Turkish-lira services and trading remained available.
notes: Temporary crypto-transfer suspension; not a shutdown marker.

title: GMX v1 / GLP was exploited for about USD 42 million
description: GMX v1 / GLP on Arbitrum was exploited for about USD 42 million, with the attacker later offered a white-hat bounty and some funds reportedly returned.
notes: Use exploit because this was a protocol/liquidity-pool exploit, not a centralized exchange wallet hack.

title: GMX disabled v1 trading and GLP mint/redeem
description: GMX disabled GMX v1 trading and GLP mint/redeem functionality on Arbitrum and Avalanche after the exploit, while stating or reporting that GMX v2 was unaffected.
notes: Temporary/partial protocol disablement; keep entity active because the broader GMX protocol continued.

title: GMX recovery process began after white-hat negotiations
description: Follow-up reporting described a white-hat bounty / return-of-funds process after the GMX exploit, supporting active classification despite the serious v1 incident.
notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no white-hat recovery enum.

title: Bithumb accidentally credited users with massive BTC balances
description: Bithumb mistakenly recorded approximately 620,000 BTC as promotional rewards in user accounts, a ledger/payout error widely reported as equivalent to tens of billions of U.S. dollars at the time.
notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no dedicated operational_error enum. This was not reported as a hack.

title: Bithumb restricted affected accounts after the crediting error
description: Bithumb restricted affected users' trading and withdrawals within about 35 minutes of the erroneous crediting incident and later recovered the vast majority of the mistaken balances.
notes: Modeled as withdrawal_suspended because the status-changing user impact involved account and withdrawal restrictions, not global exchange closure.

title: Bithumb incident prompted regulatory inspection
description: South Korean regulators and authorities were reported to be inspecting Bithumb after the erroneous BTC crediting incident exposed internal-control and operational-risk concerns.
notes: Regulatory inspection / scrutiny context; keep entity active.

title: WOO X reported unauthorized withdrawals from nine accounts
description: WOO X reported a contained incident in which nine user accounts experienced unauthorized withdrawals totaling about USD 14 million, while saying the affected users would be fully covered.
notes: Use hack as the nearest HEI enum for an unauthorized-withdrawal breach; wording should preserve that the incident was account-level and contained according to WOO X.

title: WOO X temporarily paused withdrawals
description: As a precaution after the unauthorized-withdrawal incident, WOO X temporarily suspended withdrawals while completing an internal review and coordinating with security teams and other exchanges.
notes: Temporary withdrawal pause only; not a shutdown or global trading halt.

title: WOO X committed to cover all unauthorized withdrawals
description: WOO X said affected users had been contacted and that all unauthorized withdrawals would be fully covered, supporting active-side treatment rather than dead or insolvent classification.
notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no compensation_commitment enum.

title: Crypto-Trade announced permanent shutdown
description: Crypto-Trade announced that it would shut down permanently because low volume and a weak userbase left it unable to cover server costs, staff salaries, and promotion costs.
notes: The quoted announcement gave users a short withdrawal window.

title: Crypto-Trade reached its planned offline date
description: Crypto-Trade's quoted shutdown notice said the site would go offline on 2015-01-29 at 06:00 UTC after processing pending withdrawals.
notes: HEI uses the planned site-offline date as the terminal marker.

title: SwissCEX public announcement thread opened
description: SwissCEX publicly described itself as a Switzerland-based cryptocurrency exchange operating since February 2014.
notes: Launch marker uses the public announcement thread rather than a formal company registration record.

title: SwissCEX closure process was publicly discussed
description: The SwissCEX operator referenced an exchange-closure document and said users would receive private keys for settled accounts as part of the closure process.
notes: The date follows quoted closure-process discussion in the public Bitcointalk thread.

title: SwissCEX was reported closed
description: Public forum discussion reported that the SwissCEX site had closed while users were receiving private keys for coins held on the exchange.
notes: HEI uses the public site-closed discussion as the terminal marker, supported by later inactive market-data listings.

title: CryptalDash launch marker
description: Public exchange-review coverage describes CryptalDash as a cryptocurrency exchange launched around July 2018.
notes: HEI uses a month-level launch marker from secondary exchange-review coverage.

title: CryptalDash rebranded into DLTify
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that CryptalDash was no longer active under the CryptalDash name and had rebranded into DLTify.
notes: HEI treats this as the terminal marker for the original CryptalDash exchange identity.

title: FCoin paused trading
description: FCoin paused trading after disclosing that its asset reserve had fallen short of user liabilities.
notes: CoinDesk reported that FCoin had paused trading and withdrawals while revealing the asset shortfall.

title: FCoin could no longer process user withdrawals
description: FCoin disclosed that it was unable to process user withdrawal demands because its asset reserve was below liabilities.
notes: The disclosed shortfall was estimated at roughly 7,000 to 13,000 BTC.

title: FCoin disclosed a major BTC shortfall
description: FCoin disclosed a reserve shortfall estimated at 7,000 to 13,000 BTC, creating an insolvency / withdrawal-impediment state for the exchange.
notes: HEI uses this disclosure date as the terminal marker for the FCoin exchange record.

title: CoinedUp public-use marker
description: Public forum discussion in early January 2014 shows users discussing and using coinedup.com as an altcoin exchange.
notes: HEI uses a conservative year-start marker because the current evidence shows use in early January 2014 but does not establish a precise launch date.

title: CoinedUp service reduction was publicly observed
description: Public forum discussion observed CoinedUp reduced to a small number of listed coins and speculated that the exchange might be closing or restructuring.
notes: This is not treated as a shutdown_effective event because it is community observation rather than an official terminal notice.

title: Atomic Trade went live
description: Public forum discussion described Atomic-Trade.com as a United States exchange and stated that it went live on 2014-02-05.
notes: HEI uses the explicit 2014-02-05 live-date claim from public Bitcointalk discussion.

title: Atomic Trade was bought out by Banx.io
description: Public acquisition coverage stated that Banx.io bought out Atomic-Trade.com and that Atomic-Trade.com became known as Banx.io as of 2015-02-23.
notes: HEI treats this as the terminal marker for the original Atomic Trade exchange identity.

title: LazyCoins beta launch marker
description: Public forum and trading references show LazyCoins operating as an exchange from July 2014, with the announcement thread describing a beta launch on 2014-07-21.
notes: HEI uses the beta-launch marker rather than the later official-launch publicity date.

title: LazyCoins relaunch and LazyPay expansion were reported
description: Public reporting described LazyCoins as relaunching its online exchange and expanding toward LazyPay mobile wallet and merchant services.
notes: Recorded as contextual operational expansion rather than a status transition.

title: LazyCoins official launch was reported
description: Cointelegraph reported LazyCoins' official launch as a UK-based digital currency exchange supporting GBP and euro markets.
notes: This is retained as a later official-launch publicity marker after the beta / relaunch phase.

title: 796 Exchange public launch marker
description: 796 Exchange publicly described itself as a Hong Kong-operated Bitcoin futures and finance exchange and referenced public launch / trading activity beginning around 2013-06-09.
notes: The English announcement thread describes 796 Xchange Limited, the 796.com domain, Hong Kong incorporation, and trading activity just after 2013-06-09. The Chinese announcement describes the internal test period before the public launch.

title: 796 Exchange reported 1,000 BTC withdrawal-address tampering loss
description: Public reporting described a 1,000 BTC loss at 796 after a withdrawal address was allegedly tampered with during a compromised customer-service / withdrawal process.
notes: HEI records this as a major incident, not as a shutdown marker.

title: BitKonan founded / launched
description: BitKonan is described by multiple references as a Croatia-based centralized cryptocurrency exchange founded or launched in 2013.
notes: Date is year-level. Cryptowisser and Blank describe 2013; CoinPaprika describes the exchange as running since 2014.

title: Bitcoin-24 first live
description: Bitcoin-24 is described by Bitcoin Wiki as first live in May 2012.
notes: Date is month-level. Stored as 2012-05-01 for schema consistency.

title: Bitcoin-24 shut down after bank-account closures
description: Bitcoin-24 displayed a shutdown / unavailable notice in April 2013, citing closure of its Polish bank account, while later reporting described both Polish and German account closures and legal action.
notes: Used as the terminal regulatory / banking shutdown marker.

title: Bitfloor operating since 2011
description: Bitfloor is described as a New York-based Bitcoin exchange operating since 2011, with first publicity in February 2012.
notes: Date is year-level. Stored as 2011-01-01 for schema consistency.

title: Bitfloor lost about 24,000 BTC in hot-wallet theft
description: Bitfloor suspended operations after attackers compromised servers and stole roughly 24,000 BTC from an unencrypted wallet-key backup.
notes: Major incident. Bitfloor later resumed operations, so this is not used as the terminal shutdown marker.

title: Bitfloor halted operations indefinitely
description: Bitfloor announced it would halt operations and return funds after its U.S. bank account was scheduled to be closed and it could no longer provide the same level of USD deposits and withdrawals.
notes: Used as the terminal shutdown marker.

title: Harborly founded
description: Harborly is described as a Texas-based online Bitcoin platform founded in 2014.
notes: Date is year-level. Stored as 2014-01-01 for schema consistency.

title: Harborly announced shutdown
description: Harborly said it was closing to focus on a separate project and that the closure was not caused by a hack, fraud, or security incident.
notes: Used as the terminal voluntary shutdown marker. CoinDesk published its report on 2015-08-20 and refers to a 2015-08-14 Harborly blog post.

title: Igot founded
description: Igot is described by ABC and CoinTelegraph reporting as an Australian-founded Bitcoin exchange founded in 2013.
notes: Date is year-level. Stored as 2013-01-01 for schema consistency.

title: Igot customers reported unpaid withdrawals and collapse risk
description: ABC reported that Igot appeared on the verge of collapse, with customers owed funds, months-long withdrawal failures, and the owner acknowledging difficulty paying customers back.
notes: Recorded as severe operational distress / withdrawal failure, not as a confirmed shutdown marker.

title: Bitparking exchange operating by 2012
description: Public forum and Q&A references show exchange.bitparking.com operating as a Namecoin / Bitcoin exchange by 2012.
notes: Date is year-level. Stored as 2012-01-01 for schema consistency.

title: Bitparking exchange moved toward withdrawal-only recovery
description: Forum-preserved exchange status text stated that the exchange suffered drive corruption, would be brought up for withdrawals only after balance recovery, and that the exchange would cease operation.
notes: Recorded as a severe operational failure / withdrawal-only transition, not a fully classified terminal shutdown with death_reason.

title: BTCChina founded
description: BTCChina is described as China's first Bitcoin exchange and as founded in June 2011.
notes: Date is month-level. Stored as 2011-06-01 for schema consistency.

title: BTCChina rebranded to BTCC
description: BTCC announced that BTCChina was rebranding to BTCC to support a more global identity and broader product set.
notes: Used as the terminal brand marker for the BTCChina historical record.

title: BitNZ announced
description: Bitcoin Wiki records BitNZ as announced on 2011-09-12 as a New Zealand BTC/NZD exchange.
notes: Used as the launch / public announcement marker.

title: BitNZ withdrawal deadline after closure announcement
description: BitNZ announced it would close due to New Zealand banking problems and asked customers to withdraw funds by 2017-04-15.
notes: Used as the terminal voluntary shutdown marker. The closure context is banking-access loss rather than a hack.

title: Coinrail established
description: Coinrail is described by market-data trackers as a South Korea-registered centralized exchange established in 2017.
notes: Date is year-level. Stored as 2017-01-01 for schema consistency.

title: Coinrail suffered major cyber intrusion
description: Coinrail suspended services after a cyber intrusion in June 2018, with public reporting estimating losses around $40 million in cryptocurrencies.
notes: Recorded as a major hack / suspension event. It is not used as a definitive shutdown marker because later references indicate service resumption attempts.

title: Bitcoin-Central launched
description: Bitcoin Wiki records Bitcoin-Central as launched on 2010-12-29.
notes: Used as the historical launch marker.

title: Bitcoin-Central announced regulated banking framework
description: Bitcoin-Central / Paymium announced that Bitcoin-Central would operate within a European regulatory framework with funds held at a partner financial institution.
notes: Positive regulatory / banking milestone, not a shutdown marker.

title: Bitcoin-Central relaunched after months-long closure
description: Bitcoin Wiki records that Bitcoin-Central relaunched on 2013-09-25 after being closed for roughly half a year.
notes: Recorded as a relaunch / recovery marker. This is not a terminal event.

title: Coinffeine officially launched
description: Coinffeine officially launched its decentralized peer-to-peer Bitcoin exchange in more than 70 countries.
notes: Used as the public launch marker.

title: Coinffeine active development / operations appear to have ceased
description: Later profile references describe Coinffeine as having ceased active development and operations around 2016-2017.
notes: Approximate year-level marker only. This is not treated as a definitive shutdown date.

title: GLBSE launched
description: GLBSE launched in 2011 as a Bitcoin-denominated asset and securities exchange for shares, bonds, and other Bitcoin economy assets.
notes: Date is month-level. Bitcoin Wiki records testing in April 2011 and launch soon after; Bitcoin Magazine describes launch in May 2011.

title: GLBSE abruptly closed
description: GLBSE abruptly closed in October 2012. Contemporary reporting and community explanations cite legal, AML, tax, and regulatory concerns as the core closure rationale.
notes: Used as the terminal closure marker. Some public records preserve later customer-claim and refund-process updates after the initial closure.

title: BTCQuick launched
description: Contemporary reporting described BTCQuick as launched in November 2012 and as having reached nearly $2 million in bitcoin sales by October 2013.
notes: Date is month-level. Stored as 2012-11-01 for schema consistency.

title: BitFunder launched
description: Contemporary reporting describes BitFunder as launched around December 2012 as a Bitcoin-denominated stock / securities exchange.
notes: Date is month-level. Stored as 2012-12-01 for schema consistency.

title: BitFunder ceased new trading
description: BitFunder announced that from 2013-11-14 users would no longer be able to enter new positions or sell positions on the website, with remaining bitcoin balances to be transferred later.
notes: Used as the terminal trading-shutdown marker. Later SEC and DOJ actions are retained as regulatory / fraud context.

title: LocalEthereum launched
description: LocalCryptos began in 2017 as LocalEthereum, a non-custodial peer-to-peer Ethereum marketplace.
notes: Used as the original platform launch marker.

title: LocalEthereum rebranded to LocalCryptos
description: LocalEthereum rebranded to LocalCryptos and added non-custodial peer-to-peer Bitcoin trading support.
notes: Rebrand / expansion event, not a terminal event.

title: LocalCryptos disabled new trade creation
description: LocalCryptos announced a voluntary wind-down in October 2022, with new account creation disabled after 2022-11-04 and new trade creation disabled after 2022-11-18.
notes: Used as the terminal marketplace shutdown marker. Existing wallet UI availability is not treated as continued exchange operation.
